response:
Sec. 1  
people allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their bodies are not adapted to the changes in pressure and oxygen levels that occur in water. their immune system reacts to peanuts and may cause severe reactions when exposed to water. the physical properties of water make it difficult for them to survive in aquatic environments.  

Sec. 2  
the human body is designed for air and land, not for water. the lack of oxygen in water and the changes in pressure make it hard for the body to function properly. the allergic reaction to peanuts is unrelated to the physical environment of water and does not affect the ability to live in underwater environments.
